description: Pool Tournament App — group stage round-robin into single elimination knockout

items.heading: Set up environment variables
items.description: Create the local environment variable file and commit a .env.example so any developer cloning the repo knows what to configure. Required variables: MONGODB_URI (MongoDB Atlas mongodb+srv:// connection string), NEXTAUTH_SECRET (random string — generate with `openssl rand -base64 32` — used to sign cookies and JWTs), NEXTAUTH_URL (canonical app URL: http://localhost:3000 for local dev, Vercel deployment URL in production). Google OAuth vars (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET) are out of scope until Google auth is added. Acceptance criteria: app boots locally with .env.local in place; .env.example is committed with placeholder values and a comment explaining each variable; .env.local is gitignored.
steps: Confirm .env.local is listed in .gitignore (add it if missing)
Generate NEXTAUTH_SECRET value with: openssl rand -base64 32
Create .env.example committed to the repo with placeholder values and a comment above each variable explaining what it is and how to obtain it

items.heading: Configure NextAuth.js v5 Credentials provider
items.description: Set up the authentication infrastructure using NextAuth.js v5's built-in Credentials provider. This establishes the session cookie that will later be shared with Google OAuth — no migration needed when Google is added. Includes: installing bcryptjs for password hashing, creating the Mongoose USERS model (fields: email with unique index, passwordHash, createdAt), wiring the Credentials provider into the NextAuth config, and confirming the MongoDB adapter is active. Acceptance criteria: a valid email/password pair produces a NextAuth session cookie; an invalid pair returns an auth error; passwords are never stored in plain text.
steps: Create Mongoose USERS model with fields: email (unique index), passwordHash, createdAt
Add Credentials provider to NextAuth config (auth.ts / NextAuth options) — provider validates email + bcrypt.compare against stored hash
Confirm MongoDB adapter is configured in NextAuth options
Smoke-test: signing in with valid credentials sets a session cookie; invalid credentials return an error

items.heading: Registration and login pages with session cookie
items.description: Build the registration and login UI flows that sit on top of the Credentials provider (PRD-002). Registration: email + password form, server action hashes the password with bcrypt, writes the user to the USERS collection, then calls NextAuth signIn to issue the session cookie, and redirects to /dashboard. Login: email + password form, delegates to NextAuth Credentials provider, redirects to /dashboard on success. Duplicate email on registration must show a generic error (e.g. 'Registration failed — please try again') without revealing the email already exists. The /dashboard route must be protected in proxy.ts so unauthenticated users are redirected to /login.
steps: Create /app/register page and form (email, password fields)
Create server action for registration: validate inputs, bcrypt.hash password, insert into USERS, call signIn('credentials'), redirect to /dashboard
Show generic error on duplicate email — do not reveal the email is taken
Create /app/login page and form (email, password fields)
Create server action for login: call signIn('credentials'), redirect to /dashboard on success, show error on failure
Protect /dashboard in proxy.ts — redirect unauthenticated requests to /login
Create placeholder /app/dashboard page (can be minimal — just confirms the user is logged in)

items.heading: Add display name to registration
items.description: Extend the registration flow and USERS model to capture an optional display name, used wherever player names appear in the app (doubles half-team list, group standings, bracket, etc.). Add a displayName field (string, required at DB level but pre-populated so the user never has to type it) to the USERS Mongoose model. On the registration form, show an optional 'Display name' text input that is pre-populated client-side with the email prefix (everything before the @) as the default. The server action saves whatever value is in the field — if the user clears it, fall back to the email prefix. Display name does not need to be unique. Acceptance criteria: USERS model has a displayName field; the registration form shows and pre-populates the field; the stored displayName is used everywhere a user's name is displayed; existing users created before this change are not broken (displayName can be derived from email at read time if null).

items.heading: Tournament and Team Mongoose models
items.description: Define the core data models that underpin all tournament functionality. Tournament model fields: name (string, required), mode ('singles' | 'doubles', required), status ('open' | 'in-progress' | 'complete', default 'open'), organizerUserId (ObjectId ref USERS, required), createdAt. Team model fields: tournamentId (ObjectId ref TOURNAMENT, required), userIds (array of ObjectId ref USERS, max 1 for singles, max 2 for doubles), status ('open' | 'full') where 'open' means a doubles half-team waiting for a second player, createdAt. Acceptance criteria: models are registered following the existing pattern (mongoose.models.X || mongoose.model('X', schema)); indexes on tournamentId in Team; no user can appear in more than one team per tournament (enforced at the application layer in server actions, not a DB constraint for v1).

items.heading: Dashboard with My Tournaments and Open Tournaments
items.description: Expand the placeholder dashboard (PRD-003) into a functional home screen. Two sections: 'My Tournaments' — tournaments where the logged-in user is a team member or the organizer (show name, mode, status, team count, and a View button); 'Open Tournaments' — tournaments with status=open that the user has not joined and is not the organizer of (show name, mode, team count, and a Join button). A 'Create Tournament' button appears prominently at the top. Acceptance criteria: both sections render correctly; a tournament does not appear in both sections simultaneously; an organizer who has not joined a team sees it in My Tournaments (as organizer) and not in Open Tournaments; empty state messages are shown when a section has no items.

items.heading: Create a tournament
items.description: Allow any logged-in user to create a new tournament, becoming its organizer. Form fields: Name (text, required), Mode (Singles / Doubles radio, required). On submit: a server action creates the Tournament record with status='open' and organizerUserId set to the current user, then redirects to the dashboard. The organizer is not automatically added as a team — they join separately if they want to play. Acceptance criteria: tournament appears in the creator's 'My Tournaments' section immediately; it also appears in other users' 'Open Tournaments'; name is required (show inline error if blank); mode selection is required.

items.heading: Join a tournament (singles and doubles flow)
items.description: Allow a logged-in user to join an open tournament. Singles flow: a single 'Join' button on the dashboard creates a Team record with the user's id and status='full', then refreshes the dashboard. Doubles flow: clicking 'Join' opens a page or modal showing (a) a list of open half-teams (Teams with status='open' in this tournament — show the waiting player's displayName) with a 'Join Team' button per row, and (b) a 'Start a new half-team' option that creates a Team with status='open' and the user's id. Joining an open half-team adds the user to that Team's userIds and sets status='full'. Guard: if the user already has a team in this tournament, the Join button is hidden and replaced with a 'You're in' indicator. A user may not join if the tournament status is not 'open'. Acceptance criteria: singles join is one click; doubles shows available half-teams and the new-half-team option; joining a half-team marks it full; a user cannot join the same tournament twice.

items.heading: Leave a tournament before it starts
items.description: Allow a user to leave a tournament while it is still in 'open' status. Singles: removes the user's Team record entirely. Doubles full team (status='full'): the leaving user is removed from userIds and the team reverts to status='open' so the remaining player becomes an open half-team again. Doubles half-team (status='open', only one member): the Team record is deleted. Leaving is only permitted while tournament status='open' — once 'in-progress' or 'complete', the option is hidden. Acceptance criteria: after leaving, the tournament moves from 'My Tournaments' to 'Open Tournaments' for that user; a doubles partner's half-team reappears in the open half-teams list; leaving a tournament you're not in is a no-op.

items.heading: Start a tournament (organizer action)
items.description: Allow the organizer to lock the roster and move the tournament from 'open' to 'in-progress'. A 'Start Tournament' button is visible only to the organizer on their tournament's detail or dashboard card, and only when: status='open' and there are at least 2 full teams (teams with status='full'). Clicking the button sets tournament status='in-progress', after which no new teams can join and no existing team can leave. Acceptance criteria: button is hidden from non-organizers; button is disabled/hidden if fewer than 2 full teams; after starting, Join and Leave actions are no longer available; the tournament moves to 'in-progress' status in all users' dashboards.

items.heading: Tournament detail page
items.description: A single adaptive page at /tournament/[id] that renders differently based on the tournament's current status. Open phase: shows the full team roster (team names / player display names), the organizer's 'Start Tournament' button (when conditions are met per PRD-010), and join/leave actions for the current user. In-progress phase: shows two tabs — 'Groups' and 'Bracket'. The Groups tab is active first; it shows each group as a standings table (rank, team, played, won, lost, points) plus the list of that group's matches (team A vs team B, result or 'Pending'). Each unplayed match shows a 'Record Result' button for the organizer (or delegated player per PRD-018) which opens an inline modal with two buttons: one per team, clicking a team marks them as the winner and closes the modal. The Bracket tab is visible but locked (greyed, non-interactive) until the group stage is complete; once complete it unlocks and shows the knockout bracket with the same inline 'Record Result' mechanic on each unplayed match. Complete phase: the Bracket tab is shown by default with all results filled in and the winning team prominently highlighted. All users (organizer and players) can view the full detail page; only the organizer or delegated players can record results. Acceptance criteria: the page renders correctly for all three phases; tab switching works; the Record Result modal correctly updates the match and triggers a standings/bracket refresh; the page is accessible to any authenticated user; the correct tab is shown by default for each phase.

items.heading: Determine tournament path on start
items.description: When the organizer starts a tournament (PRD-010), the system must immediately determine whether the tournament runs a group stage or goes straight to knockout, based on the number of full teams. Rule: 4 or fewer full teams → straight to knockout; 5 or more full teams → group stage first. This determination is made once at start time and stored on the Tournament record (add a path field: 'group-stage' | 'direct-knockout'). Acceptance criteria: tournaments with ≤4 full teams set path='direct-knockout' and skip group stage; tournaments with ≥5 full teams set path='group-stage'; the path field is set atomically when status transitions to 'in-progress'; no manual override needed.

items.heading: Generate groups and round-robin schedule
items.description: For tournaments with path='group-stage' (≥5 teams), split full teams into groups and generate all round-robin matches within each group. Grouping: distribute teams as evenly as possible across groups (target 3–4 teams per group, typically 4 groups). Assignment is random. For each group, generate a match record for every unique team pair (round-robin: n*(n-1)/2 matches per group of n teams). Match model fields: tournamentId, groupId (or null for knockout), teamAId, teamBId, winnerId (null until result recorded), phase ('group' | 'knockout'), round (null for group stage), delegatedToTeamId (null by default — see PRD-018), createdAt. Store group membership on a Group model: tournamentId, name (e.g. 'Group A'), teamIds[]. Acceptance criteria: all group matches are generated at once when the group stage is created; no team plays itself; every team plays every other team in their group exactly once; groups and matches are readable from the tournament detail page.

items.heading: Record group stage results and standings
items.description: Allow the organizer (or a delegated team — PRD-018) to record win/loss results for group stage matches, and display live standings per group. Recording: for each group match, a 'Record Result' action accepts the winning teamId, sets match.winnerId, and is idempotent (re-recording overwrites the previous result). Standings: calculated on the fly from match results — Win = 1 point, Loss = 0 points. Where two or more teams are level on points within a group, resolve the tie using the head-to-head result between those specific teams. Display: each group shows a standings table (rank, team name, played, won, lost, points) and the list of matches with result or 'pending'. Acceptance criteria: recording a result updates standings immediately; tiebreaker is applied correctly; re-recording a result is allowed and updates standings; only the organizer or delegated team can submit results.

items.heading: Advance top 2 teams from each group
items.description: Once all group stage matches have results recorded, identify the top 2 teams from each group (by points, tiebreaker applied), mark the group stage complete, and produce the list of knockout qualifiers. The system should detect when all group matches have a winnerId and automatically surface an 'Advance to Knockout' action for the organizer to confirm (no silent auto-progression). Acceptance criteria: the action is only available when all group matches have results; the correct 2 teams per group advance (with tiebreaker applied); the list of qualifiers is stored and feeds into PRD-016 bracket generation; teams that did not advance see their tournament journey end.

items.heading: Generate knockout bracket
items.description: Generate the single-elimination knockout bracket. Input: for path='group-stage', the qualifiers from PRD-015; for path='direct-knockout', all full teams. Seeding is random — no cross-group seeding logic. If the number of teams does not fill a clean power-of-2 bracket, randomly assign byes; a team with a bye automatically advances to the next round. Bracket structure: determine rounds based on team count (e.g. 8 teams → QF, SF, Final; 4 teams → SF, Final; 2 teams → Final). Generate match records for the first knockout round (phase='knockout', round='QF'/'SF'/'Final' as appropriate), with teamBId=null for bye slots. Acceptance criteria: bracket is generated in one operation; byes are randomly and fairly distributed; the bracket structure correctly reflects the team count; first-round matches are visible on the tournament detail page.

items.heading: Record knockout results and advance winners
items.description: Allow the organizer (or delegated team — PRD-018) to record win/loss results for knockout matches, automatically advancing the winner to the next round and eliminating the loser. When a result is recorded for a knockout match, create the next-round match record if both slots for that match are now filled (i.e. both feeder matches have results). Continue until the Final is played. When the Final result is recorded, set tournament.status='complete' and store the winning teamId on the Tournament record. Acceptance criteria: recording a result advances the winner to the next bracket slot; the losing team is eliminated; the bracket display updates to show the winner; when the Final is resolved, the tournament is marked complete and a winner is displayed; re-recording a knockout result is allowed (overwrites and recalculates downstream if needed — for v1 this is acceptable complexity to skip; just allow correction before the next round is played).

items.heading: Delegate match result entry to players
items.description: Allow the tournament organizer to delegate result recording for a specific match to the teams playing in it, removing the bottleneck of the organizer recording every result. Delegation is per-match: the organizer can toggle delegation on any unplayed match (group stage or knockout). When delegated, either player in the match's teams can record the result for that match. The organizer retains the ability to record or override any match result at any time regardless of delegation state. Acceptance criteria: organizer sees a 'Delegate' toggle/button on each unplayed match; when delegated, players in that match see a 'Record Result' action; non-delegated matches show no record action to non-organizer players; the organizer can always record or correct any result.

items.heading: Global nav bar with user identity and logout
items.description: Add a persistent header across all authenticated pages showing who is logged in and providing a logout action. The header displays the user's displayName, falling back to their email if displayName is not set. A 'Log out' button calls NextAuth's signOut() and redirects to /login. Implementation requires introducing a Next.js route group (e.g. src/app/(authenticated)/) with a shared layout that renders the header above all authenticated pages (dashboard, tournament pages, etc). The public pages (landing, login, register) are excluded from this layout and keep their current bare appearance. Acceptance criteria: the header is visible on every authenticated page; the displayed name is displayName if set, otherwise email; clicking 'Log out' ends the session and redirects to /login; the header is not shown on /login or /register.

items.heading: Allow organizer to join and play their own tournament
items.description: Remove the restriction that prevents a tournament organizer from joining their own tournament as a player. Currently the canJoin guard in the tournament detail page explicitly excludes the organizer (!isOrganizer). This should be removed so the organizer goes through the exact same join and leave flow as any other player — including the doubles half-team lobby. The organizer retains all management capabilities (recording results, delegating, starting the tournament) regardless of whether they have joined as a player. Acceptance criteria: the organizer sees the Join button on their own tournament when it is open and they have not yet joined; the organizer can join a singles tournament in one click; the organizer can join a doubles tournament via the half-team lobby (join an open slot or start a new half-team); the organizer can leave before the tournament starts; the Start Tournament button still appears (for the organizer) independently of whether they have joined as a player.

items.heading: Show organizer name on dashboard tournament cards
items.description: Display the organizer's display name on every tournament card in the dashboard — both the My Tournaments and Open Tournaments sections. Fetch the organizer user's displayName (falling back to email prefix if not set) for each tournament and render it as a small metadata line on the card, e.g. 'Organised by Carl'. This gives players context on who is running a tournament before they join, and reminds organizers which of their tournaments are theirs. Acceptance criteria: both My Tournaments and Open Tournaments cards show the organizer name; the name is the organizer's displayName if set, otherwise their email prefix; the label reads something like 'Organised by [name]'; the existing Organizer badge (shown only to the organizer themselves) is kept alongside this new label.

items.heading: Dashboard cards — player count and created date
items.description: Add two metadata items to each tournament card on the dashboard (both My Tournaments and Open Tournaments sections). (1) Player count: total number of players currently in the tournament, counting singles players and both members of any doubles team — shown as '8 players'. For open half-teams (doubles status='open'), count only the single member present. (2) Created date: the tournament's createdAt date formatted as 'Created 2 Apr' (day and abbreviated month only, no year). Acceptance criteria: both metadata items appear on every card in both sections; player count reflects all enrolled players not just full teams; the date format is 'Created D Mon' with no leading zero on the day.

items.heading: Join page — show full teams and redirect to tournament
items.description: Two improvements to the tournament join flow. (1) The doubles join page (/tournament/[id]/join) currently lists only open half-teams (status='open'). It must also display already-formed full teams (status='full') so players can see who is already in the tournament — these are shown as read-only, without a join button. (2) After any successful join action (singles join, doubles join-half-team, doubles start-half-team), redirect the user to /tournament/[id] rather than /dashboard, so they land directly on the tournament detail page and can see their registration in context. Acceptance criteria: the join page shows a 'Full teams' section listing all status='full' teams by player name(s); open half-teams are still listed with their join button; the new full-teams section is read-only; after joining, the user is sent to /tournament/[id].

items.heading: Team names for doubles tournaments
items.description: Allow doubles teams to optionally set a team name. Singles tournaments are unaffected. When a player starts a new half-team via the join flow, an optional 'Team name' text input (max 50 chars) is shown alongside the existing join UI — the player can leave it blank. Once a team is formed (status='full' or 'open'), either member can rename the team inline on the tournament detail page roster tab, via a small edit affordance next to the team name — this is only available while the tournament status is 'open'; once 'in-progress' or 'complete' the name is locked and the edit control is hidden. The organizer cannot rename other teams. Display: wherever a team is shown (roster tab, standings table, bracket, join page open/full team lists), if the team has a name, display it as '{TeamName} (Player1 & Player2)' — the team name followed by the player names in parentheses. If no name is set, fall back to the existing display logic (just 'Player1 & Player2') unchanged. For open half-teams with a name, display as '{TeamName} (PlayerName, looking for partner)'. Model change: add an optional name field (string, max 50 chars) to the Team Mongoose model. Acceptance criteria: optional name input appears when starting a new half-team; name is saved on team creation; either team member sees an inline edit control on the roster tab while the tournament is open; editing saves the new name and refreshes the display; when a name is set it displays as '{Name} (players)' everywhere a team is shown; no name set = existing fallback display; singles tournaments and non-team-member users see no edit controls.
